versions.installHtml:
# Publish the k8gb service template inside the k0rdent control plane.
helm upgrade --install k8gb oci://ghcr.io/k0rdent/catalog/charts/kgst \
  --set "chart=k8gb:v0.19.0" \
  -n kcm-system

# Create DNS provider credentials in each target cluster when ExternalDNS is enabled.
kubectl create namespace k8gb --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
kubectl -n k8gb create secret generic dns-credentials \
  --from-literal=CF_API_TOKEN="${CF_API_TOKEN}"
versions.verifyHtml:
kubectl get servicetemplates -A
# NAMESPACE    NAME                            VALID
# kcm-system   k8gb-v0-19-0                    true
versions.deployHtml:
apiVersion: k0rdent.mirantis.com/v1beta1
kind: MultiClusterService
metadata:
  name: k8gb
spec:
  clusterSelector:
    matchLabels:
      group: demo
  serviceSpec:
    services:
      - template: k8gb-v0-19-0
        name: k8gb
        namespace: k8gb
        values: |
          k8gb:
            k8gb:
              dnsZones:
                - parentZone: example.com
                  loadBalancedZone: cloud.example.com
                  dnsZoneNegTTL: 30
              edgeDNSServers:
                - 1.1.1.1
              clusterGeoTag: us-east-1
              extGslbClustersGeoTags: eu-west-1
              reconcileRequeueSeconds: 30
            extdns:
              enabled: true
              provider:
                name: cloudflare
              domainFilters:
                - example.com
              txtPrefix: k8gb-us-east-1-
              txtOwnerId: k8gb-cloud-example-us-east-1
              env:
                - name: CF_API_TOKEN
                  valueFrom:
                    secretKeyRef:
                      name: dns-credentials
                      key: CF_API_TOKEN

prerequisitesHtml:
• A delegated DNS zone for k8gb to manage.
  • Network access from each target cluster to the parent DNS resolver.
  • DNS provider credentials when enabling the bundled ExternalDNS integration.

examples.title: Route53-backed multi-region failover
examples.contentHtml: 1. Bootstrap two clusters (e.g. EKS in eu-west-1 and us-east-1) and federate them with k0rdent. 2. Reuse the sample values from docs/examples/route53/k8gb/ to set dnsZones, edgeDNSServers, and IRSA annotations for ExternalDNS. 3. Apply the Route53 example Gslb (docs/examples/route53/k8gb/gslb-failover.yaml) to each cluster and watch kubectl -n test-gslb get gslb report which region is active. 4. Scale the frontend deployment in the primary region to zero and verify traffic fails over by curling the delegated FQDN (see docs/deploy_route53.md for command snippets).
